action: In Visual Studio Code, click the 'File' menu, then click 'Open Folder…'. In the file picker GUI, navigate to the 'Documents' folder, then select the 'CalculatorApp' folder and confirm to open it.
fallback_action: Perform a Google search using Internet Explorer

recipe.steps: is the first action. The task is to open a folder in Visual Studio Code. The screen shows Visual Studio Code is open. To begin, I need to click the 'File' menu to access the option to open a folder.

previous action: I was instructed to click the 'File' menu in Visual Studio Code. The current screen shows that the File menu is open, displaying a dropdown with options such as 'New Text File', 'New File', 'New Window', 'Open Folder', etc. This confirms the previous action succeeded. The next step is to click the 'Open Folder...' option to open the file picker dialog.

previous action: I was instructed to click 'Open Folder...' in the File menu. The current screen shows the Windows file picker dialog, which confirms that the previous action succeeded. The next step is to navigate to the 'Documents' folder, which is commonly found in the left sidebar of the file picker. I will look for and click on the 'Documents' folder in the sidebar to proceed.

previous action: I was instructed to click the 'Documents' folder in the file picker sidebar. The current screen now shows the contents of the 'Documents' folder, as indicated by the highlighted 'Documents' in the sidebar and the folder contents displayed in the main area. The next step is to locate and select the 'CalculatorApp' folder within 'Documents'. I will look for the 'CalculatorApp' folder and click on it to select it.

previous action: I was instructed to click on the 'CalculatorApp' folder in the Documents directory. The current screen shows the 'CalculatorApp' folder is now highlighted, indicating it is selected. The next step is to confirm the selection and open the folder. In the Windows file picker, this is typically done by clicking the 'Select Folder' button, which is usually located at the bottom right of the dialog. I will look for and click the 'Select Folder' button to proceed.
